steps.title: Analyze the exponential equation
steps.content:
Rearrange to isolate the exponential expression.
$(\frac{a}{4})^{x+c}=\frac{b}{7}$
Because $a\gt4$, the base is greater than $1$. A positive-base exponential function takes each positive value exactly once. Since $b$ is positive, $\frac{b}{7}$ is positive, so there is one intersection. The answer is B.
